headline: Builders and Software Lead a Broadly Positive Session as Chips Drag
body_html:
Equities are holding modest gains at midday Friday, with advancing issues outpacing decliners 218 to 177 across a 396-name universe. The session's tone is constructive but uneven: homebuilders are the day's clearest winner, up roughly 1%, followed by software at 0.81% and consumer names at half a point. Materials are quietly contributing as well, while energy edges fractionally higher despite a complicated geopolitical backdrop in the Middle East.

The dominant drag is semiconductors, off 1.87% and easily the worst-performing pocket of the market. The pressure is broad within the group — Allegro MicroSystems, Marvell Technology, and Teradyne are each shedding more than 3.7% — even as investors appear to be rotating selectively rather than fleeing risk outright. Healthcare is also in the red, down 0.36%, keeping overall gains in check.

On the upside, Nutanix is the standout mover of the session, surging 5.04% to lead all gainers. Weyerhaeuser and Boise Cascade, both timber-linked names, are adding more than 4% apiece, amplifying the materials-and-housing thread running through today's trade. Into the close, watch whether the semiconductor selloff deepens or stabilizes — that sector's direction may set the tone for the week's final print.

briefs.headline: Nutanix Surges to Session-Best Gain on Cloud Momentum
briefs.body: Nutanix is advancing 5.04% midday, making it the top performer across the tracked universe. The cloud-infrastructure software name is riding a broader bid for enterprise software, a group that is itself up 0.81% — the second-strongest sector on the day. No company-specific catalyst has been flagged, but the move is drawing attention.

briefs.headline: Timber and Wood-Products Names Cluster at Top of Leaderboard
briefs.body: Weyerhaeuser and Boise Cascade are each rising more than 4%, lifting the materials complex alongside homebuilders. The paired move suggests housing-supply optimism is filtering into upstream wood-products names. Homebuilders, the top sector at 1.04%, are providing the demand-side context for the rally.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Pressure Persists Across Sub-Groups
briefs.body: Chips are the session's clear laggard, falling 1.87% as a sector. Allegro MicroSystems, Marvell Technology, Teradyne, and Vicor Corporation are each off between 3.68% and 4.22%, spanning automotive semis, networking silicon, test equipment, and power modules. The breadth of weakness suggests sector-level rotation rather than a single stock-specific event.

briefs.headline: Oil on Course for Weekly Advance as Hormuz Risks Weigh on Supply Outlook
briefs.body: Crude is heading toward a weekly gain as shipping activity through the Strait of Hormuz slows amid rising U.S.-Iran tensions. The International Energy Agency has cautioned that an escalation could jeopardize a projected 2027 oil-market surplus. Prediction-market participants are separately pricing in sustained elevated gasoline costs.

briefs.headline: Gold Slips on Rate-Hike Anxiety Tied to Geopolitical Flare-Up
briefs.body: Bullion is on track for a weekly loss even as geopolitical uncertainty typically supports safe-haven demand. Analysts note that Iran-related tensions are simultaneously stoking inflation fears, which in turn are reviving rate-hike speculation and weighing on non-yielding assets like gold.

briefs.headline: Small-Cap Stocks Draw Attention From Momentum-Seeking Traders
briefs.body: With large-cap leadership concentrated in a handful of names, some market participants are turning to smaller-capitalization equities in search of the next significant move. The rotation interest comes as a notable options transaction on the Nasdaq 100 has caught the eye of bullish traders, adding to a constructive but selective mid-session mood.
